protocol_definition.name: Sequential Handoff
protocol_definition.visibility: Each agent sees the task and upstream outputs only; no backward messages.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after one forward pass and Writer output.

messages.content: You are Planner. Decompose the task, identify deliverables, constraints, and a concise execution plan. Do not fabricate external evidence.

You are acting as role: Planner.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After a file is uploaded, the system should:

1. Validate the file type and size.
2. Store the original file and its metadata.
3. Extract the document text.
4. Divide the text into searchable sections.
5. Create a searchable index.
6. Allow users to search the processed documents.
7. Report the processing status of each uploaded file.

The design must also support the following requirements:

- Files may be processed asynchronously.
- A failed processing step should be retryable.
- Repeated uploads of the same file should not create unnecessary duplicate records.
- New document formats should be easy to add later.
- The design should remain understandable and maintainable for a small development team.

Produce a software design that explains the system components, data flow, API interface, data model, code organization, error-handling strategy, and testing plan.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, or code examples may be included where they help clarify the design.

Required Output Format:
Use the following structure:

1. System overview
2. Main components and responsibilities
3. End-to-end processing workflow
4. REST API design
5. Data model
6. Suggested project structure
7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
8. Testing plan
9. Design tradeoff

Include the following two tables.

### Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|

The response should be approximately 700–900 words.

Visible prior context:
No prior agent output is visible.

Instruction:
Create a concise execution plan and deliverable structure.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.
You are Researcher. Gather and organize evidence available in the prompt and common conceptual knowledge. Respect the task tool requirement.

You are acting as role: Researcher.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After a file is uploaded, the system should:

1. Validate the file type and size.
2. Store the original file and its metadata.
3. Extract the document text.
4. Divide the text into searchable sections.
5. Create a searchable index.
6. Allow users to search the processed documents.
7. Report the processing status of each uploaded file.

The design must also support the following requirements:

- Files may be processed asynchronously.
- A failed processing step should be retryable.
- Repeated uploads of the same file should not create unnecessary duplicate records.
- New document formats should be easy to add later.
- The design should remain understandable and maintainable for a small development team.

Produce a software design that explains the system components, data flow, API interface, data model, code organization, error-handling strategy, and testing plan.

final_output:
1. System overview
This design describes a backend for a document-processing and search service that accepts PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. Files can be uploaded and processed asynchronously. Each upload results in stored originals, extracted text, and a searchable index. The system supports retries for failed steps, deduplicates repeated uploads, and is extensible to new formats with minimal changes. The architecture emphasizes maintainability for a small team.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Ingress | Authenticate, validate requests, route to services | Upload requests, search queries | Status responses, search results |
| File Upload Service | Validate file type/size, deduplicate, persist metadata, enqueue processing | File bytes, metadata | Upload record, processing job created |
| Metadata Store | Persist file metadata, deduplication keys, processing status | Metadata from uploads | Metadata records, IDs |
| Processing Orchestrator | Coordinate asynchronous processing steps, retries, failure handling | Processing jobs | Task results, updated statuses |
| Text Extraction Service | Extract raw text from accepted formats | Original file storage reference | Document text, per-page/section data |
| Document Splitter / Sectionizer | Divide text into searchable sections (chapters, paragraphs, sections) | Full document text | List of sections with spans/offsets |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Build and update a searchable index; support queries | Document sections, metadata | Search index entries |
| Storage Layer | Persist originals, extracted text, sections, and index structures | File data, text, sections | Durable storage |
| Duplication & Versioning Handler | Detect repeated uploads, avoid duplicates, manage versioning | File fingerprint, metadata | Reference to existing record or new record |
| Status & Monitoring Service | Provide processing status per upload; alert on failures | Processing state | Status summaries, metrics |
| Admin/Orchestrator UI or API | Optional tooling for viewing status and logs | Admin requests | Status dashboards, logs |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload phase
  - Client sends file (PDF/TXT/Markdown) with optional metadata (filename, user ID, tags).
  - API validates file type and size; computes a content fingerprint (e.g., hash of file bytes) for deduplication.
  - If a duplicate exists, return existing document reference without creating a new record; otherwise create a new upload record and store metadata.
  - Enqueue a processing job (asynchronous) with reference to the stored file.

- Processing phase (orchestrated)
  - Processing Orchestrator triggers steps in order:
    1) Validation step (redundant but guarantees constraints).
    2) Text extraction service extracts raw text from the stored file depending on format.
    3) Section splitter analyzes extracted text into logical sections with positions.
    4) Indexer builds/updates a search index for the sections and associates with document metadata.
  - Each step is idempotent and retryable. If a step fails, the orchestrator retries up to a configurable limit with backoff. If persistent failure, mark as failed and emit a status event.

- Post-processing
  - Upon success, update document status to "processed" and provide a reference to the searchable index and document metadata.
  - Users can issue search queries; the system returns relevant sections with document references and metadata.

- Re-upload handling
  - On each upload, compute a fingerprint. If a duplicate exists, reuse the existing document record, and return its status; do not duplicate the index or text if already present.

- Extensibility
  - New formats plug-in: add a new extractor module and register in the processing pipeline without changing core orchestration.

4. REST API design

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload a new document | file (multipart), userId, optional metadata | uploadId, status, documentRef (if duplicate) |
| GET | /uploads/{uploadId} | Get processing status and metadata | uploadId | status, meta, progress, errors (if any) |
| GET | /documents/{documentId}/index | Retrieve index metadata for a document | documentId | indexId, size, lastIndexedAt |
| POST | /search | Execute a text search | query string, optional filters (documentId, tags) | list of results (documentId, snippet, sectionInfo) |
| GET | /documents/{documentId} | Retrieve extracted text and sections (read-only) | documentId | full text, sections with offsets |
| GET | /health | System health | - | status, metrics |

5. Data model (high level)

- UploadRecord
  - uploadId: string
  - userId: string
  - filename: string
  - mimeType: string
  - size: int
  - fingerprint: string
  - status: enum {PENDING, PROCESSING, SUCCESS, FAILED}
  - createdAt, updatedAt
  - documentId: string (link to the deduplicated document, if exists)

- Document
  - documentId: string
  - fingerprint: string
  - originalFilename: string
  - mimeType: string
  - size: int
  - createdAt
  - status: enum {READY, PROCESSED}
  - indexId: string (reference to search index)

- TextContent
  - documentId: string
  - plainText: string
  - extractedAt: timestamp

- Section
  - sectionId: string
  - documentId: string
  - index: int
  - heading (optional): string
  - text: string
  - startOffset, endOffset: int

- IndexEntry
  - indexId: string
  - documentId: string
  - sectionId: string
  - contentSnippet: string
  - vectorData (optional for vector search): blob or references
  - weight: float

- ProcessingLog
  - uploadId/documentId
  - step: string
  - status: enum {SUCCESS, FAILED, RETRYING}
  - message
  - timestamp

6. Suggested project structure

- cmd/
  - api-server/ (REST API entry point)
- internal/
  - api/ (HTTP handlers, request/response models)
  - storage/ (blob/file storage interfaces)
  - model/ (data models)
  - pipeline/ (orchestrator, steps)
  - extractors/ (pdfTxtMd extractor implementations)
  - splitter/ (sectionization)
  - index/ (search index builder and query)
  - dup/ (deduplication logic)
  - status/ (status and metrics)
  - config/ (feature flags, settings)
- pkg/
  - messaging/ (queue abstractions)
  - error/ (custom errors)
- tests/
  - unit/, integration/
- docs/
  - design.md, runbooks

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Retry strategy
  - Each processing step is idempotent and retryable with exponential backoff.
  - Use a centralized retry policy: maxRetries, backoffBase, jitter to avoid thundering herd.
  - Persist step status per-upload; on transient failure, mark as RETRYING and requeue.

- Failure handling
  - If a step fails beyond maxRetries, mark UploadRecord as FAILED and emit a failure event plus alert hook for operators.
  - Include error codes and human-readable messages in ProcessingLog for diagnostics.

- Duplicate prevention
  - Compute a fingerprint (hash) of file bytes on upload.
  - Look up existing Document by fingerprint; if found, link UploadRecord to existing Document and return existing documentRef.
  - Do not duplicate original file storage or index entries for repeated uploads of identical content.

- Extensibility for new formats
  - Define an Extractor interface with a single method extract(fileReference) -> ExtractResult.
  - Add new extractor implementations without altering core orchestration logic.
  - Register extractors by MIME type or file extension in a configuration map.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Validate fingerprinting, deduplication, and metadata storage logic.
  - Test extractors with sample PDF/TXT/Markdown inputs (mocking external libraries).
  - Verify sectionizer produces deterministic sections for given text.

- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow from POST /uploads to processing completion, including successful indexing and search results.
  - Retry path: simulate transient failures in a step and ensure retries occur with backoff and eventual success or proper failure.

- Contract tests
  - HTTP API contracts for /uploads, /search, /documents endpoints; validate required fields, error responses, and response schemas.

- Performance and scale tests
  - Bulk uploads with varying sizes; measure throughput of extraction, indexing, and search latency.
  - Verify deduplication performs correctly under concurrent uploads of identical files.

- Observability tests
  - Verify ProcessingLog, status endpoints, and health checks reflect accurate state transitions.

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous processing vs. synchronous responses
  - Pros: better throughput, scalable processing, non-blocking UX.
  - Cons: users must poll or subscribe for status; eventual consistency design required.

- Deduplication strategy
  - Pros: storage efficiency, avoids duplicate indexing.
  - Cons: fingerprint collisions risk; requires content-addressable storage approach and careful cache invalidation.

- Pluggable extractors
  - Pros: easy to add formats; clear separation of concerns.
  - Cons: requires stable API and proper versioning of extractor interfaces.

- Indexing vs. full-text scan
  - Pros: fast search results; supports ranking by sections.
  - Cons: more storage and maintenance for index; vector search can add complexity.

Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / Ingress | Authenticate, validate, route | Upload requests, search queries | Status responses, results |
| File Upload Service | Validate type/size, dedupe, persist metadata, enqueue | File bytes, metadata | Upload record, processing job |
| Metadata Store | Persist file metadata, dedupe keys | Metadata | Metadata records, IDs |
| Processing Orchestrator | Coordinate steps, retries, failure handling | Processing jobs | Task results, updated statuses |
| Text Extraction Service | Extract text from formats | Original file storage | Document text, per-segment data |
| Document Splitter / Sectionizer | Create searchable sections | Document text | Sections with offsets |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Build/update search index; support queries | Sections, metadata | Index entries |
| Storage Layer | Persist originals, text, sections, index | File data, text, sections | Durable storage |
| Duplication & Versioning Handler | Detect duplicates; manage versions | File fingerprint | Reference to document or new record |
| Status & Monitoring Service | Provide per-upload status; metrics | Processing state | Status summaries, alerts |
| Admin/Orchestrator UI/API | View status and logs | Admin requests | Dashboards, logs |

API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload a new document | file (multipart), userId, metadata | uploadId, status, documentRef (if duplicate) |
| GET | /uploads/{uploadId} | Get processing status | uploadId | status, meta, progress, errors |
| GET | /documents/{documentId}/index | Retrieve index metadata | documentId | indexId, size, lastIndexedAt |
| POST | /search | Execute a text search | query, optional filters | results: list of {documentId, snippet, sectionInfo} |
| GET | /documents/{documentId} | Retrieve extracted text & sections | documentId | full text, sections |
| GET | /health | System health | - | status, metrics | 

Design notes: This design favors clarity and maintainability for a small team. It emphasizes a modular pipeline with clear interfaces, robust error handling, and straightforward extension points for new document formats and indexing capabilities.

agent_effective_config.description: Low-latency OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.
